# qfs server guide

`qfs serve <config.qfs>` runs the server from a config file. A config is just a list of `create â€¦` bindings â€” each one takes a query you already know and runs it on an event, a schedule, or an HTTP request. Bindings use the same language as everything else (see the [language reference](language.md)), so you can preview exactly what a binding would do before installing it.

## Bindings (`create â€¦`)

| binding | shape | what causes the plan to run |
|---------|-------|-----------------------------|
| `create endpoint` | `endpoint <name> do <stmt>` | an HTTP `fetch` to the endpoint route |
| `create trigger` | `trigger <name> on <event> do <stmt>` | an event/watcher firing |
| `create job` | `job <name> every <interval> do <stmt>` | the cron scheduler / Cron trigger |
| `create view` | `[materialized] view <name> as <pipeline>` | a query against the view |
| `create webhook` | `webhook <name> do <stmt>` | a signed inbound `/hooks/...` POST |
| `create policy` | `policy <name> <predicate>` | (gate) blocks plans that violate it |

## Policy & least privilege

`create policy` gates writes by verb / path / irreversibility â€” an irreversible effect (`remove`, `call mail.send`) can be blocked or required to be explicitly acknowledged. **Credentials are never inline in a config**: a binding references a stored connection by handle (`qfs connection add <driver> <name>`), and no token is ever written to a config, a log, or a generated doc. Examples below use placeholder handles only.

```qfs
create endpoint recent on 'GET /recent' as /mail/inbox |> limit 5
create trigger notify on /mail/inbox do insert into /slack/acme/general/messages values (NEW.subject)
create job nightly every '1h' do remove /tmp/scratch where age > 7
create policy api ALLOW select DENY insert, update, remove, call
```

## Deployment targets

The same bindings deploy onto two production hosts behind the `RuntimeHost` seam (ADR-0005). `qfs` *documents* the mapping here; ticket **t36** *builds* the host adapters:

| binding | EC2 daemon (`host-daemon`) | Cloudflare Workers (`host-workers`, parked) |
|---------|---------------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| `endpoint` | the qfs-http listener | Worker `fetch` |
| `job` | the qfs-cron interval daemon | Cron Trigger |
| `webhook` / event | the watchtower bus + `/hooks/...` ingest | Queues |
| watcher / `LAST_RUN` | the on-disk durable store | Durable Object |
| `/d1` Â· `/r2` Â· `/kv` | (native only on Workers) | native bindings |

The Workers host is honestly **parked** (the worker crate is offline in this build); the EC2 daemon is the live target. See ADR-0005 and ADR-0007 for the offline/disk scoping.
